source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A coherent plane wave of wavelength $\lambda$ is incident upon a screen with an infinitely long slit of width $d$. The beam's propagation direction is perpendicular to the slit and makes an angle $\theta$ with the normal to the screen (Fig. 2.32). Find the intensity distribution beyond the slit in the focal plane of a thin lens of focal length $f$. [Assume the lens, diameter infinite.]

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A coherent plane wave of wavelength $\lambda$ is incident upon a screen with an infinitely long slit of width $d$. The beam's propagation direction is perpendicular to the slit and makes an angle $\theta$ with the normal to the screen (Fig. 2.32). Find the intensity distribution beyond the slit in the focal plane of a thin lens of focal length $f$. [Assume the lens, diameter infinite.]
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains PIL object representations rather than explicit file paths or URLs
question references Fig. 2.32, but the record marks vision_relevance as optional
metadata.reasoning_chain:
Using the coordinates shown in Fig. 2.34, the incident plane wave is represented by
$$
e^{i \frac{2\pi}{\lambda} y' \sin\theta}.
$$

The amplitude distribution in the focal plane of the lens is given by the Fraunhofer diffraction pattern of the slit:
$$
\begin{aligned}
A &\propto \int_{-\frac{d}{2}}^{\frac{d}{2}} e^{i \frac{2\pi}{\lambda} \sin\theta\, y'} e^{-i 2\pi f_y y'} dy' \\
&\propto \frac{\sin\left[\pi\left(\frac{\sin\theta}{\lambda} - f_y\right) d\right]}{\pi\left(\frac{\sin\theta}{\lambda} - f_y\right) d}
\end{aligned}
$$
with
$$
f_y = \frac{y}{\lambda f}.
$$

Hence, the intensity distribution is
$$
I = a^2 \frac{\sin^2\left[\pi\left(\frac{\sin\theta}{\lambda} - \frac{y}{\lambda f}\right) d\right]}{\left[\pi\left(\frac{\sin\theta}{\lambda} - \frac{y}{\lambda f}\right) d\right]^2},
$$
where $a$ is a constant.

The principal maximum occurs when:
$$
\frac{\sin\theta}{\lambda} - \frac{y}{\lambda f} = 0 \quad \text{or} \quad y = f \sin\theta.
$$

The minima occur when:
$$
\pi\left(\frac{\sin\theta}{\lambda} - \frac{y}{\lambda f}\right) d = k\pi \quad \text{or} \quad y = f \sin\theta - \frac{k\lambda f}{d},
$$
where $k = \pm 1, \pm 2, \ldots$.
